{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Professor Birch
|pkmn=Mudkip
|type1=Water
|img=May Mudkip.png
|epnum=AG001
|epname=Get the Show on the Road!
|vajp=
|vaen=Lindsey Warner
|desc=When [[Professor Birch]] was attacked by {{p|Poochyena}}, May borrowed {{p|Mudkip}} from his bag to fight the wild Poochyena. However, May's lack of experience in commanding Pokémon led to Mudkip attacking her instead, resulting in Professor Birch having to fend off the Poochyena himself. Later, it reappeared as one of the possible [[first partner Pokémon]] for May, but the previous bad experience led her to not choose it.

It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[AG025|A Mudkip Mission]]'', when May wanted to catch another one.

Mudkip's only known move is {{m|Water Gun}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Jessie
|pkmn=Wurmple
|type1=Bug
|gender=female
|img=May Jessie Wurmple.png
|epnum=AG014
|epname=All in a Day's Wurmple
|vajp=Chinami Nishimura
|vaen=Tara Jayne
|desc=In ''[[AG021|Which Wurmple's Which?]]'', May argued with [[Jessie]] over which one of them had the better {{p|Wurmple}}. After [[Max]] concluded that the two Wurmple were exactly the same, they began battling, but Team Rocket was sent blasting off by {{AP|Pikachu}}. May soon realized that she has Jessie's Wurmple and that Jessie has hers. Eventually, Wurmple was returned to May in exchange for Jessie's Wurmple.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Kyle Hamm
|pkmn=Hitmonchan
|gender=male
|type1=Fighting
|img=May Hitmonchan.png
|epnum=AG145
|epname=Pasta La Vista!
|vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi
|vaen=Michael Haigney
|desc=May borrowed {{p|Hitmonchan}} from [[Kyle Hamm]] after [[Master Hamm]] broke his back.

Hitmonchan's known moves are {{m|Comet Punch}}, {{m|Mach Punch}}, {{m|Agility}}, {{m|Focus Punch}}, and {{m|Sky Uppercut}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Matt
|pkmn=Larvitar
|type1=Rock
|type2=Ground
|img=Matt Larvitar.png
|epnum=AG188
|epname=Pinch Healing
|vajp=Chinami Nishimura
|vaen=Sarah Natochenny
|desc= May befriended {{p|Larvitar}} during its stay at the [[Fennel Valley]] [[Pokémon Center]], trying to help it overcome its shyness. After {{TRT}} captured Larvitar and its friends, May commanded it to use {{m|Sandstorm}} to to free everyone from the balloon.

Larvitar's only known move is {{m|Sandstorm}}.}}
